sources_audited.note: The cited Census profile resolves and its URL/title identify NAICS 334514, but the page exposed only a JavaScript shell in this audit, so the listed product examples could not be independently inspected on the cited page.
BLS reports NAICS 334500 real output of $149.7 billion in 2024, $184.7 billion in 2034, and a 2.1% compound annual growth rate.
The May 2023 BLS OEWS table for NAICS 334500 reports 17.58% architecture and engineering, 26.89% production, and 7.77% office and administrative support employment.

NIST describes manufacturing AI use cases including quality control, document management, support, forecasting, scheduling, and inspection, and identifies data, cost, skills, cybersecurity, and legacy-system barriers. It is broad manufacturing evidence rather than a measurement of 334514 exposure or adoption.
EPA describes the progression from manual water-meter reading to AMR and real-time AMI, including billing-frequency, leak-detection, and data-management implications.

The EPA-hosted report's text supports a large installed US water-meter base, gradual replacement of legacy meters by AMR/AMI, operational benefits, and utility funding constraints. Its market statistics are old and water-specific.

The Census graphic reports 51% of responding owners of employer businesses were age 55 or older, based on the 2019 Annual Business Survey for data year 2018, and explicitly warns that the percentages do not represent all US business owners.

caveats: S1's cited Census profile was reachable only as a JavaScript shell during the audit; the code and title were visible, but the specific product examples were not independently inspectable there.
S6 is directionally relevant but water-specific and relies on market and installed-base statistics largely from 2010-2011, limiting its value for a current six-digit demand estimate.

S4 validates broad manufacturing AI use cases and barriers but does not measure task exposure or five-year implementation in NAICS 334514; the packet appropriately treats the resulting inputs as PROXY or ESTIMATE.

Eligibility, transfer probability, and benefit retention remain unmeasured judgments across a heterogeneous NAICS basket; these weaknesses are disclosed and correctly keep the action at VALIDATE_ASSUMPTIONS.
summary: Mechanics pass. The record is useful and internally coherent, and its broad proxies and unmeasured judgments are labeled and caveated honestly. Reopened sources support the registered directional claims and quoted statistics except that the JavaScript-only Census profile allowed only partial verification. No source or construct issue passes the required materiality test: correcting or downgrading these limitations would not change the LOW_PRIORITY base tier or the MODEL_CONDITIONED action category.
